pullRequest.title: docs: clarify GitHub webhook authentication support
pullRequest.body:
# Summary

Updates the GitHub change hook documentation to clarify that requests are not authenticated by default and that authentication can be configured using a webhook secret or change_hook_auth.

Fixes #3104.


# Contributor Checklist:

* [ ] I have updated the appropriate documentation

pullRequest.title: fix(www): display deleted log message instead of empty content
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- When the Janitor deletes old log chunks, it sets `log.type = 'd'` but keeps the log record with its original `num_lines`. The UI had no handling for type `'d'`, so deleted logs appeared as empty content with stale "view all N lines" links and non-functional download buttons
- Added type `'d'` handling in both `LogViewer` (full-page view) and `LogPreview` (inline build summary):
  - **LogViewer**: early return with a message when the log has been deleted
  - **LogPreview**: skip content fetching, show "deleted" in the card header, show deletion message in the expanded body, hide download buttons

## Components changed

| Component | Change |
|---|---|
| `LogViewer.tsx` | Early return with message for `type === 'd'` |
| `LogPreview.tsx` | Guard in useEffect to skip content fetch; deleted message in body; "deleted" label in header replacing view/download links |

## Test plan

- [x] `LogViewer` returns a message for deleted logs, preventing `LogViewerText` from attempting to render empty content
- [x] `LogPreview` useEffect skips API calls for deleted logs, avoiding unnecessary requests
- [x] `LogPreview` header shows "deleted" instead of stale "view all N lines" link and download buttons
- [x] `LogPreview` body shows clear deletion message when expanded
- [x] Non-deleted logs (`s`, `t`, `h`) are unaffected — all type checks are additive
- [x] `LogView.tsx` doesn't need changes — it delegates to `LogViewer` which now handles type `'d'`
- [x] `BuildSummary` still lists deleted logs (correct — hiding them would confuse users)

Fixes #4274

fixes #5211

all other client commands encoding username and password, but `[master/buildbot/clients/usersclient.py](https://github.com/buildbot/buildbot/compare/master...chripo:buildbot:5211-encode-before-hashing#diff-12f66770fd1abd970e4b0245c9cd4885fbde687bba116f41725514bdc86d0e39) didn't got the changes in d1b9f032e581a7167d65cf25953d726658e51629

This works as long as you hardcode the credentials, but it fails when retrieving them via secrets.
The test cases do not take the credentials into account because of the `self._fake_login`. :/

pullRequest.title: fix(www): add missing eventKey to NestedParameter tabs layout
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- `NestedParameter` with `layout="tabs"` was non-functional - clicking tabs showed no content
- The `<Tab>` components were missing the `eventKey` prop required by react-bootstrap to track the active tab
- Without `eventKey`, react-bootstrap never activated any tab panel, so all content stayed hidden
- Added `eventKey` on each `<Tab>` and `defaultActiveKey` on `<Tabs>`, matching the pattern used by `BuildView`, `WorkerView`, and `BuilderView`

## Root cause

When the AngularJS ForceBuildModal was rewritten in React, the tabs layout in `FieldNested.tsx` used `<Tab key={...}>` (a React reconciliation hint) but omitted `eventKey` (the react-bootstrap tab identifier). All other `<Tabs>` usages in the codebase (`BuildView`, `WorkerView`, `BuilderView`, `BuildRequestView`) correctly set `eventKey`. PR #7571 (referenced in #8138) did not touch `FieldNested.tsx`.

## Test plan

- [x] `eventKey` type (`string | number`) matches react-bootstrap API
- [x] `defaultActiveKey` correctly computed from first visible field's name/index
- [x] `defaultActiveKey={undefined}` is safe when all fields are hidden (renders empty tabs bar, same as before)
- [x] Original array indices from `filteredMap` are unique and consistent with `defaultActiveKey` computation
- [x] Pattern matches all other `<Tabs>` usages in the codebase

This likely also fixes #8138 (same root cause - tabs layout not displaying content).

Fixes #8316

pullRequest.title: Fix build badge properties missing in Grid, Console, Masters, and Debug views
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Build badges in Grid View, Console View, Masters View, and Build View debug tab were always showing just the build number, ignoring the `Links.build_link_template` setting (e.g. `%(prop:branch) (%(build_number))`)
- These four views never passed the `property` query parameter when fetching builds via `Build.getAll()`, so `build.properties` was always `{}` and template placeholders like `%(prop:branch)` resolved to empty strings
- Added `property: getBuildLinkDisplayProperties()` (or spread into existing arrays) to the `Build.getAll` queries, matching the pattern already used by `BuilderView`, `WorkerView`, `WorkersView`, `ChangeBuildsView`, and `BuildersTable`

## Root cause

When builds are fetched from the Buildbot REST API, properties are only included in the response if explicitly requested via the `property` query parameter. `BuildLinkWithSummaryTooltip` calls `formatBuildLinkText(build)` which reads `build.properties[prop]`, but the four affected views never requested the properties, so they were always empty.

## Test plan

- [x] All four views now use `getBuildLinkDisplayProperties()` — the same utility and pattern used by `BuilderView`, `WorkerView`, `WorkersView`, `ChangeBuildsView`, and `BuildersTable`
- [x] `getBuildLinkDisplayProperties()` is re-exported from `buildbot-ui` (via `main.ts`) — no new dependency needed
- [x] ConsoleView preserves `'got_revision'` in the property array (needed by `selectChangeForBuild` for change-to-build mapping)
- [x] Calling `getBuildLinkDisplayProperties()` inside `useDataApiQuery` callbacks matches established codebase pattern
- [x] When `Links.build_link_template` is empty, `getBuildLinkDisplayProperties()` returns `[]`, so no unnecessary properties are fetched

Fixes #8142

pullRequest.title: Fix ChoiceStringParameter custom value paste and edit with strict=False
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- `ChoiceStringParameter` with `strict=False` (single-select) used `CreatableSelect` from react-select, which did not support pasting, editing, or provide any visual indication that custom values are accepted
- Replaced `CreatableSelect` with a native `<input type="text">` backed by a `<datalist>` for the `strict=False, multiple=False` case
- Paste, in-place edit, and free-text input now work natively
- The browser-rendered datalist dropdown makes it visually clear that suggestions exist while the text input makes it clear that custom values are accepted

## What changed

- `strict=True` (default): unchanged — uses `Select` from react-select
- `strict=False, multiple=True`: unchanged — uses `CreatableSelect` from react-select
- `strict=False, multiple=False`: now uses `<Form.Control type="text" list={...}>` with a `<datalist>` instead of `CreatableSelect`

## Test plan

- [x] Existing tests all pass — they use `strict: true` (default) and don't hit the changed branch
- [x] `state.value` null case handled via `?? ''` fallback
- [x] `datalist` is well-supported in all modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
- [x] `fieldsState.setValue` is called on every `onChange`, keeping state in sync
- [x] `strict=True` and `multiple=True` code paths are completely unchanged

Addresses the three complaints from #8155:
1. Cannot paste — **fixed**: native text input fully supports paste
2. Cannot edit — **fixed**: native text input supports in-place editing
3. No indication the field accepts custom values — **fixed**: the field is now a visible text input with datalist suggestions

Fixes #8155

pullRequest.title: fix(console_view): show avatar and timestamp in console view
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- The React console view had `compact={true}` hardcoded on the `ChangeDetails` component, which hid both the user avatar and the relative timestamp
- The original AngularJS console view never set the `compact` attribute, so it defaulted to `undefined` (falsy) and always showed avatars
- Changed `compact={true}` to `compact={false}` to restore the original behavior

## Root cause

When the console view was ported from AngularJS to React (commit 986bc329d), `compact={true}` was set on `ChangeDetails` without matching the old behavior. In the AngularJS version, `compact` was an optional binding (`=?`) that defaulted to `undefined`, so avatars and timestamps were always visible in the console view.

## Test plan

- [x] Verified the fix matches behavior described by the maintainer in the issue
- [x] Confirmed no other `ChangeDetails` callers have the same bug
- [x] Confirmed no existing tests reference the compact prop in ConsoleView
- [x] Reviewed CSS/layout - the 20px avatar fits within the existing row header width

Fixes #8156

pullRequest.title: feat(grid_view): read and write branch filter from URL query param
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- The grid view branch selector was local React state only — linking to a specific branch (e.g. `/#/grid?branch=3.13`) was not possible
- Replaced `useState` with `useSearchParams` so the branch filter is synced to the URL query parameter `branch`
- Selecting "(all)" removes the `branch` param to keep URLs clean
- Follows the same `useSearchParams` pattern already used by `useTagFilterManager` for the `tags` param

## Test plan

- [x] Navigating to `/#/grid?branch=main` pre-selects "main" in the branch dropdown
- [x] Changing the branch dropdown updates the URL with `?branch=<value>`
- [x] Selecting "(all)" removes the `branch` param from the URL
- [x] Existing `tags` query param is preserved when changing branches (both use spread-copy pattern)
- [x] Back/forward browser navigation works (setSearchParams defaults to push)
- [x] Branch names with special characters are handled by URLSearchParams encoding
- [x] Loading state is unaffected — LoadingIndicator renders before the select is shown

Fixes #8324

pullRequest.title: scripts: fix cleanupDatabase not an async function
pullRequest.body:
on older Twisted (like 23.10.0), defer.maybeDeferred would not handle coroutines that return a deferred. Newer seems to handle it.

In any case, this is an obvious mistake.

Fixes #8370

This is not really an issue on master since Twisted >=24.7.0 is now required, which handle this.
So might be a need to backport?
Not sure I should add a newsfragments or not since the bug no longer exists on master.

## Contributor Checklist:

* [n/a?] I have created a file in the `newsfragments` directory (and read the `README.txt` in that directory)

pullRequest.title: monkeypatches: Work around old workers not being able to connect
pullRequest.body:
The cause of the error is this new code in Twisted:

https://github.com/twisted/twisted/commit/2ac17b0efd15f828214bb54ac5dee8a99b4df75e#diff-3ee4b290d7221a2e082e7c4c040b64321158a7fdb26bfdb5aefebe4c54075e78R488-R492

Old workers may not send parents attribute in their state, thus they will fail to connect. The same problem may happen on newer workers as well, but it will only be triggered when an exception is sent to the master.

Fixes: https://github.com/buildbot/buildbot/issues/8374

pullRequest.title: .github: add release workflow
pullRequest.body:
On publish of a new Release, this will be triggered to build tarballs and pyinstaller worker for linux and windows. They will be added to the release that triggered them.

This remove appveyor and circleci jobs.

Closes #8412 

The release process might be even more automated, by triggering on a new tag and creating the release.
The pypi upload could also be trivially added here.

This remove the codecov from appveyor and circleci, but is codecov still really used?

This also add back the pyinstaller build of buildbot-worker for windows.

Here is an example run on my fork: https://github.com/tdesveaux/buildbot/releases/tag/v99.9.9
Related action: https://github.com/tdesveaux/buildbot/actions/runs/15014550509
The release was simply created by adding a title and tag, the action built and added the files.

## Contributor Checklist:

* [n/a] I have created a file in the `newsfragments` directory (and read the `README.txt` in that directory)

pullRequest.title: fix(www): set document.title from topbar items on each view
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Since the React migration (Buildbot 4.0.0), every page title was stuck on "Buildbot" because the old AngularJS `document.title` logic was never ported
- This hooks into `useTopbarItems` — already called by all major views with breadcrumb captions — to also set `document.title` from those captions, prefixed with the configured site title
- On view cleanup, the title resets to just the site title (e.g. "Buildbot")
- Empty captions from loading states are filtered out to avoid orphan separators

## Examples

| View | Title |
|---|---|
| Builders list | `Buildbot: Builders` |
| Builder page | `Buildbot: Builders › my-project › my-builder` |
| Build page | `Buildbot: Builders › my-project › my-builder › 42` |
| Projects list | `Buildbot: Projects` |
| Non-topbar views (Home, Settings, etc.) | `Buildbot` (falls back to site title) |

## Root cause

The AngularJS app set `document.title` per-route via `$state.current.data.pageTitle` templates. The React rewrite replaced routing with `useTopbarItems` for the in-app navbar breadcrumbs, but never added the corresponding `document.title` side effect. A commented-out tombstone at `BuildView.tsx:322-324` confirms the gap was known.

## Design decisions

- Reuses the existing `useTopbarItems` hook rather than adding per-view title logic — every view that already calls `useTopbarItems` automatically gets correct page titles
- Follows the same pattern as `useFavIcon` in the codebase (side-effect hook that reads config and updates a DOM property)
- Uses `›` as breadcrumb separator in the title, consistent with common web conventions
- Cleanup effect resets `document.title` to `config.title` when navigating away, so views that don't call `useTopbarItems` (like Settings, Home) fall back to the base title

## Test plan

- [x] Verified ConfigContext is always available above components that call useTopbarItems (component tree in index.tsx)
- [x] Checked all 7 useTopbarItems call sites — all produce meaningful captions
- [x] Verified cleanup correctly resets title on navigation to non-topbar views
- [x] Filtered empty captions to handle loading states in LogView gracefully
- [x] Confirmed no race conditions during route transitions (cleanup runs before mount-effect, both synchronous)

Fixes #8703

pullRequest.title: Fix #8712 worker proto behind haproxy
pullRequest.body:
fixes #8712 

Twisted's `strports.listen` is not compatible with the `haproxy:` prefix to a connection string.
This change switches the dispatcher's use of `listen` to `service`.

pullRequest.title: www: Fix favicon missing due to double slash in icon URL path
pullRequest.body:
buildbotUrl is guaranteed to end with '/' by the backend (copy_str_url_param_with_trailing_slash), but FavIcon.ts concatenated  '/icon.png' and '/icon.svg', producing double slashes like 'http://host//icon.png' which results in 404.                                               Remove the leading '/' from icon path literals so that the URL is correctly formed. 
 Fixes #8722                                                                                                                                  

# Contributor Checklist: 
 [x] I have updated the unit tests                                                                                                          
 [x] I have created a file in the `newsfragments` directory (and read the `README.txt` in that directory)

pullRequest.title: www: restore default_page configuration support
pullRequest.body:
The default_page setting was lost during the AngularJS to React migration.
  Re-implement it by adding a resolveDefaultPage utility that validates the
  configured value and redirects from "/" to the specified page using React
  Router's Navigate component.

  Changes:
  - Add default_page field to the frontend Config type
  - Add resolveDefaultPage() with validation (empty string, protocol-relative URLs, URI schemes)
  - Replace the "/" route with a Navigate redirect when default_page is configured
  - Add frontend unit tests (9 cases) and backend unit test

  Fixes #8758

  # Contributor Checklist:

  * [x] I have updated the unit tests
  * [x] I have created a file in the `newsfragments` directory (and read the `README.txt` in that directory)

pullRequest.title: Address ReDoS, OOM and blocking issues in WarningCountingShellCommand
pullRequest.body:
Addressing the concerns raised in (:issue:`8819`), several improvements have been made to `WarningCountingShellCommand`. A safer regex for suppression lines replaces the previous one to eliminate backtracking risks. Streaming the suppression file to a temporary location instead of loading it entirely into memory prevents potential OOM on the master. Offloading the file parsing to a separate thread ensures the main event loop remains responsive during processing, which I believe is critical for master stability.

Verified the changes against existing tests and updated the expectations to account for the switch from `StringFileWriter` to `FileWriter`.

# Contributor Checklist:
- [x] Unit tests have been updated
- [x] Newsfragment has been created

Closes #8819.
